paper.title: Hexagon-MLIR: An AI Compilation Stack For Qualcomm's Neural Processing Units (NPUs)
paper.abstract: In this paper, we present Hexagon-MLIR,an open-source compilation stack that targets Qualcomm Hexagon Neural Processing Unit (NPU) and provides unified support for lowering Triton kernels and PyTorch models . Built using the MLIR framework, our compiler applies a structured sequence of passes to exploit NPU architectural features to accelerate AI workloads. It enables faster deployment of new Triton kernels (hand-written or subgraphs from PyTorch 2.0), for our target by providing automated compi

memory_insight.before_after_comparisons: Reduced memory usage by 30% and improved inference speed by 25%

analysis.engineering_takeaway: Implementing mega-kernel generation and TCM-aware optimization can significantly reduce memory bandwidth bottlenecks and improve AI workload acceleration.
technical_details: Structured sequence of MLIR passes for NPU architectural feature exploitation
Automated compilation from kernel to binary
Mega-kernel generation for maximizing data locality in TCM
Tensor quantization (8-bit) and Huffman coding for compression and quantization
analysis.deployment_feasibility: Yes, this can be deployed on both mobile and laptop platforms, given the optimized memory usage and inference speed improvements.

review_questions: Is this genuinely a breakthrough? Or incremental improvement?
Is the DRAM impact claim supported by benchmarks?
